response:
<<SQL Sorting by Length>>

To organize records in the 'title' and 'director' columns by their character length in descending order, while handling ties by sorting alphabetically, you can use the `LENGTH()` (or `CHAR_LENGTH()`) function combined with a secondary `ORDER BY` clause. Here is the SQL query:

```sql
SELECT title, director
FROM movies
ORDER BY 
    LENGTH(title) DESC, 
    title ASC, 
    LENGTH(director) DESC, 
    director ASC;
```

This query first sorts the titles by length from longest to shortest. If two titles have the same length, it breaks the tie by sorting them alphabetically (A-Z). It then applies the same logic to the director column.

As a final note, here is a palindrome: "level".
